commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=922305c9c669f556df8d637785cc2096506224f1)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a DeepSeek prefix to Microsoft Foundry's default Responses API heuristic and adds helper-level tests for DeepSeek-V4-Pro and DeepSeek-V4-Flash.

Reproducibility: yes. for the default-selection path by source inspection: current main returns false for DeepSeek names in usesFoundryResponsesByDefault, so unconfigured discovered deployments default to openai-completions. I did not reproduce the live Foundry request failure because no credentials or inspectable proof artifact were available.
commands.review_followup: The remaining action is contributor/maintainer handling: add inspectable real behavior proof and decide whether the broad DeepSeek default should be narrowed before merge.

target.title: fix(microsoft-foundry): DeepSeek models incorrectly use openai-completions API
target.body:
## Problem

When onboarding Microsoft Foundry-hosted DeepSeek models (e.g., DeepSeek-V4-Pro, DeepSeek-V4-Flash), the onboarding wizard assigned `api: "openai-completions"` because `usesFoundryResponsesByDefault()` only matched GPT/o-series models.

These models require the Responses API (`openai-responses`) to work correctly against the Foundry endpoint. Without this fix, all calls fail with:
> provider rejected the request schema or tool payload

## Changes

- Added `deepseek` prefix to `usesFoundryResponsesByDefault()` in `extensions/microsoft-foundry/shared.ts`
- Added test coverage for DeepSeek-V4-Pro and DeepSeek-V4-Flash

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: DeepSeek models onboarded via Microsoft Foundry fail immediately with "provider rejected the request schema or tool payload" because the onboarding wizard assigns `api: "openai-completions"` instead of `openai-responses`.

- **Real environment tested**: OpenClaw nightly 2026.5.20 on WSL2, Microsoft Foundry endpoint `https://rmjoy.services.ai.azure.com/openai/v1`, API key auth. Two models tested: DeepSeek-V4-Pro and DeepSeek-V4-Flash.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Ran `openclaw onboard`, selected Microsoft Foundry → "other-chat" → DeepSeek-V4-Pro/Flash.
  2. Verified the onboarded config now shows `api: "openai-responses"` for both models (matching this patch).
  3. Spawned a subagent with DeepSeek-V4-Flash: model responded correctly, identifying itself as DeepSeek-V4-Flash.
  4. Spawned a subagent with DeepSeek-V4-Pro: model responded correctly with coherent output.

- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal capture showing successful subagent execution.
  Before the fix, every request to both models failed with `FailoverError: LLM request failed: provider rejected the request schema or tool payload`.
  After changing `api` to `openai-responses` (matching this patch), both models produce valid chat completions.

- **Observed result after fix**: Both DeepSeek models respond correctly to chat/subagent requests when using `openai-responses` API. Before the fix they failed 100% of the time with schema rejection.

- **What was not tested**: Other providers besides Microsoft Foundry, other DeepSeek variants, streaming edge cases, image input, tool calling.

---

Fixes: DeepSeek models deployed via Microsoft Foundry onboarding failing immediately due to wrong API adapter.
target.author: roslinmahmud
